notes.statement: The earlier baseline arbitrary-rotation study optimized arbitrary SU(2) block targets rather than an ideal x-axis SQR gate.
The earlier residual-Z follow-up used only one mid-sequence X_pi pulse, so it did not test the requested half-SQR -> pi -> half-SQR -> pi construction.

notes.conclusion: A direct ideal SQR requires Theta_n = theta_n and Phi_n = 0 for every active manifold.
notes.equation: U_n^dir approx exp[-i (Theta_n X + Phi_n Z)/2], with Theta_n = int Omega_n dt and Phi_n = int delta_n dt.

notes.conclusion: A symmetric echoed sequence cancels first-order Z-type phase if the two halves accumulate the same Phi_n.
notes.equation: U_n^echo approx exp[-i ((Theta_{n,1}+Theta_{n,2}) X + (Phi_{n,1}-Phi_{n,2}) Z)/2] when X_pi is ideal and the target axis is X.

notes.statement: Once the inserted X_pi pulses become manifold dependent, the clean toggling-frame cancellation is spoiled and the echoed protocol can fail even when the ideal first-order algebra is favorable.
